title: The yes that changes the trajectory
subtitle: A short visualisation for the felt sense of saying yes to a good opportunity without delay.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation practice from Day 35 of the MONEY program. The scene: an opportunity arrives and instead of the usual hesitation, you feel clear and say yes immediately.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation for the felt sense of opportunity met with readiness.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to build the felt sense of meeting opportunities with clarity rather than delay.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from Day 35 of the MONEY program. The scene is not the opportunity itself — it is the moment of your response.
Sit quietly. Close your eyes. Take a slow breath in and let it go.
Picture an opportunity arriving. An email, a message, a conversation. Something that is genuinely good — a client, a project, a door you have been hoping would open.
You read it. And instead of the usual delay — the second-guessing, the checking if you are ready, the searching for what might go wrong — you feel clear. The answer is yes. You already know it.
Notice what that clarity feels like in your body. Where it sits. How it is different from the familiar hesitation.
You send your yes. Something begins. Picture the first step that follows — a reply, a meeting, a date in the diary. One concrete detail of what the yes opens.
Stay with the felt sense of having said yes clearly and quickly. Then open your eyes.
Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation for the felt sense of opportunity met with readiness.
